message:
fix(h2): RFC 7540/7541 conformance hardening and HPACK security fixes

connection.rs — Multiple RFC 7540 correctness fixes:

  process_data: Check idle-stream state BEFORE track_stream_id() so that
  a rejected DATA frame on an idle stream does not advance last_stream_id
  (which would make the invalid stream appear legitimate in GOAWAY).
  Decrement connection-level recv_window BEFORE the stream-level
  recv_data() call. The peer counted these bytes against their send
  window when transmitting, so even if the stream rejects the data (e.g.
  StreamClosed), the connection window must still account for them —
  otherwise the two sides' flow-control windows desynchronize. Adds
  data_on_closed_stream_still_decrements_connection_window test.

  process_rst_stream: Now returns Result<> and enforces two RFC rules:
  §5.1 (RST_STREAM on idle stream → connection PROTOCOL_ERROR) and
  §6.4 (RST_STREAM with stream ID 0 → connection PROTOCOL_ERROR).
  Previously RST_STREAM on an unknown/idle stream was silently accepted.
  Adds test_rst_stream_on_idle_stream_is_connection_error,
  test_rst_stream_on_open_stream, and
  test_rst_stream_on_stream_zero_is_connection_error tests.

  process_push_promise: Tighten state check per §5.1 — PUSH_PROMISE is
  only valid on streams in Open or HalfClosedLocal state (not just
  "not closed"). Upgrade unknown-stream rejection from stream error to
  connection error (§5.1 mandates connection-level PROTOCOL_ERROR). Adds
  push_promise_rejected_on_half_closed_remote_stream test. Updates
  existing test assertion for unknown-stream from stream_id=Some to None.

frame.rs — Validate SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH value per §6.5.2: values
  other than 0 or 1 must be rejected as PROTOCOL_ERROR. Adds
  test_settings_frame_rejects_invalid_enable_push_value.

hpack.rs — Multiple RFC 7541 conformance and security improvements:

  DynamicTable: Switch backing store from Vec to VecDeque. The HPACK
  dynamic table requires frequent push_front (newest entry at index 0)
  and pop_back (oldest entry eviction). Vec::insert(0, ...) is O(n);
  VecDeque::push_front is O(1) amortized.

  Encoder: Track pending_size_update. When set_max_table_size() is
  called, the encoder now emits a dynamic table size update instruction
  (§6.3) at the start of the next header block — both encode() and
  encode_sensitive() paths. Previously the table was resized internally
  but the peer was never notified on the wire, causing decoder/encoder
  table state to diverge. Adds emit_pending_size_update helper and
  three roundtrip tests.

  Decoder: Enforce §4.2 mid-block size update rejection. Dynamic table
  size updates are now only accepted before the first header field
  representation in a block. After the first header, any 0x20-prefixed
  byte triggers COMPRESSION_ERROR. Adds three tests (before-first ok,
  after-first rejected, multiple-before-first ok).

  decode_integer: Fix checked arithmetic to use checked_mul on the
  multiplier instead of relying solely on checked_shl. checked_shl
  validates shift < bit_width but does NOT catch silent truncation when
  the resulting value exceeds the platform's usize on 32-bit targets.
  Using 1usize.checked_shl(shift) then checked_mul catches the actual
  value overflow. Adds test_integer_decode_checked_mul_overflow.

  decode_string: Add MAX_STRING_LENGTH (256 KB) DoS guard that rejects
  strings before allocation, bounding memory use before the header-list-
  size check runs. Adds boundary and over-limit tests.

stream.rs — Three correctness fixes:

  send_data/recv_data: Reject DATA frames on streams in ReservedLocal
  or ReservedRemote state per §5.1, which only permits HEADERS,
  RST_STREAM, and PRIORITY in reserved states. Previously can_send()
  returned true for ReservedLocal, allowing DATA through incorrectly.
  Adds reserved_local_rejects_send_data and
  reserved_remote_rejects_recv_data tests.

  reset: Clear header_fragments and pending_data buffers immediately on
  stream reset so memory is released right away, rather than lingering
  until the stream is pruned from the store. Adds
  reset_clears_header_fragments_and_pending_data test.

  set_initial_window_size: Skip closed streams when applying SETTINGS
  window size updates. A closed stream with a deeply negative send
  window could trigger a spurious overflow error that blocks the entire
  SETTINGS frame processing. Adds
  set_initial_window_size_skips_closed_streams test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
